items.text: Notice: The bike racks outside Weller Hall will be removed on Tuesday, June 9, for sidewalk repairs. Bicycles left after 8:00 AM will be relocated to South Lot. A $10 fee is charged to reclaim any impounded bicycle from Facilities.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how much does reclaiming an impounded bicycle cost?
questions.explanation: The notice states that a $10 fee is charged to reclaim any impounded bicycle, which option D restates as a ten-dollar reclaiming fee.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a bicycle still parked at the racks at 9:00 AM on June 9?

options.A: It will remain safely in its original spot
options.B: It will be moved to a different area
options.C: It will be repaired along with the sidewalk
options.D: It will be returned to its owner for free

questions.explanation: Bicycles left after 8:00 AM are relocated to South Lot, so a bike still there at 9:00 AM would be moved elsewhere.

items.text: Notice: The recycling bins currently located in the Morrison Hall lobby will be moved to the loading dock behind the building on Monday, March 2. Residents are asked to deposit paper and plastic there beginning that day. Questions may be directed to Mr. Ellis.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where will the recycling bins be placed?

options.D: Inside a locked basement storage room

questions.explanation: The notice says the bins will be moved to the loading dock behind the building, which option A restates as the rear loading dock.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about residents who currently recycle in the lobby?

options.A: They will no longer be permitted to recycle
options.B: They will use a different drop-off point
options.C: They will pay a small recycling fee
options.D: They will sort their trash more carefully

questions.explanation: Since the bins are relocating from the lobby to the loading dock, lobby recyclers will have to bring their materials to the new spot.

items.text: Notice: The north elevator in Carver Hall will be out of service from Wednesday, April 8, through Friday, April 10, for scheduled maintenance. During this period, please use the south elevator or the main stairwell. We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when will the north elevator be unavailable?

options.A: For a single day on April 8
options.D: Throughout the entire month of April

questions.explanation: The notice says the elevator will be out of service from April 8 through April 10, which option C restates as April 8 until April 10.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should people use instead?

options.A: The staircase or the other elevator
options.B: The freight elevator in the basement
options.C: The elevator in the neighboring building
options.D: The accessibility ramp near the rear entrance

questions.explanation: The notice directs people to use the south elevator or the main stairwell, which option A restates as the staircase or the other elevator.

items.text: PSA for finals week: the library basement is the quietest study spot on campus! Barely anyone knows about it. The vending machine down there sells coffee for just $1, and the study carrels stay open until 2 AM Sunday through Thursday. Bring a sweater!

questions.stem: According to the post, what does the vending machine offer?

options.A: Free hot beverages during finals week
options.B: Snacks priced at several dollars each
options.C: Tea and coffee for two dollars

questions.explanation: The post says the vending machine sells coffee for just $1, which option D restates as coffee for one dollar.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about studying in the basement late on a Wednesday night?

options.A: You could stay only until the library closes
options.B: You could run into crowds of students
options.C: You could work there well past midnight
options.D: You could owe a small entry fee

questions.explanation: The carrels stay open until 2 AM Sunday through Thursday, and Wednesday falls in that range, so a student could keep working past midnight.
